profile.practiceBio:
Karlie is a curator and art advisor based in Vietnam. Working across projects such as managing the Au O Collection, Permanently closed gallery, and MoT+++/A.Farm, where she focuses on curatorial development, artist representation, and art sales.

Her practice bridges curatorial research and the commercial art world, supporting artists through strategic placements, collector relationships, and long-term market growth while contributing to the shaping of contemporary collections in the region.
profile.welcomeBio:
La Astoria is a 21st century salon — two stories with high ceilings and a balcony, so everything feels open and expansive. This space is hybrid office-living, with occasional social gatherings: dinners, movie screenings, performances.

The first floor living room is multifunctional — a long table that shifts between work and dinner, an area for artist studio, a kitchen bar, wooden furniture, French Vietnamese tiles, chandeliers, and a lot of plants. There's also a library upstairs with the other bedroom.

It's eccentric but composed, full of energy with a little bit of drama. Work and play coexist. A crystal singing bowl for existential spirals, chandeliers, plants. There is a dalmatian sometimes.
profile.collaboration:
I'm most interested in exchange. The space functions like a salon, so conversation, critique, and shared thinking are central. I'd like an ongoing dialogue around each other's work and others' — talking through ideas, giving feedback, sharing what we're each seeing and experiencing in our different art contexts and systems. It's also about figuring out how to support each other in making work that feels aligned and necessary.

On a more practical level, I can offer connections to artists and institutions I'm in touch with, and take you to local places — markets, schools, temples, and experiences of daily life I've carried from childhood in Vietnam, like gathering items for rằm ceremony. I'm happy to support through writing, whether that's helping shape a proposal, thinking through a project, or offering feedback and perspective.
profile.neighbourhood: District 2, Thu Duc, Ho Chi Minh City
profile.environment: Urban. Two stories with high ceilings and a balcony.
profile.transport: Short walk to bus 88 (to District 1) and bus 153 (to Thảo Điền).
profile.amenities: Markets, pharmacies, and everyday essentials in the immediate area.
profile.rules: Take your own trash out. Quiet hours 2am–9am.

profile.practiceBio:
I am an artist from Alaska. I have been living in Ho Chi Minh City since 2018. I primarily make paintings but love to experiment: video, text-based work, photography, digital art. I have a familiarity with the local scene and a lot of life experience in this city.

I have been working at MoT+++ for the past year, managing the residency and other programs. I look forward to sharing this place with you!
profile.welcomeBio:
I welcome you to my glorious space, A-lát-xca studio, of which I am very proud. This is a stilt house, constructed over the river's edge. Water hyacinth floats by, the river level rises and falls throughout the day, herons and kingfishers flop around.

I have been working here for 3 years and in the past year expanded the space to include an attached apartment. This space is a great place from which to deploy my little motorboat and to explore the various winding waterways that weave throughout the urban area. Also a great place to catch a catfish or watch the sunset.

I have found the space to be immensely productive and have produced many great works here. It is a space where you don't have to worry about making a mess with paint and things dry quickly with all the moving air.

The studio is open air, with a bar and many couches for your use. I invite you to lounge around and enjoy the tropical splendor of my unique space. The view is panoramic, looking westward with trees and nature surrounding.

This space is one I have developed over time but unfortunately at some point in the future it will be demolished to make way for riverside stability projects. So while it won't be available forever, it is here now and it is alive!

If we are paired, I will happily take you out on boat excursions several times a week. There is little I enjoy more than floating around and gazing at the rich, high-resolution shores.

The accommodation side is simple and rustic, perfect for the adventurous. With so much open space and the natural cooling effect of the river, heat is manageable. I offer private space and your choice of two rooms.

The area is very charming and local, two minutes walk from Fatima Church, a big pink cathedral. If desired, there is a city bus 5 minutes walking from here and it takes you south, directly into the city center.
profile.collaboration: I am very open and looking for excuses to expand my practice. I hope we can come up with whatever it is we are bound to create by way of divine inspiration. I look forward to the challenge and am ready to adapt to the moment and to combine forces towards ultimate art, something neither of us could make alone. I hope we can devote the necessary time and attention to make something overwhelming and good; for the sake of mankind and future generations!
profile.neighbourhood: Hiep Binh Chanh, Thu Duc, Ho Chi Minh City
profile.environment: Open air, on the river's edge.
profile.transport: 5 minutes walk to a bus line running directly to the city center.
profile.amenities: 2 minutes walk from Fatima Church, a large pink cathedral.
profile.rules: When leaving, all doors and windows must be locked completely. Turn off all lights and bring valuables into the apartment area. No excessive noise at night — the studio is otherwise available around the clock. Please be respectful to the couple who rent the space. Note that the space is not built with accessibility in mind: some areas have low ceilings and there are a few steps. Health insurance is strongly recommended.

profile.practiceBio:
Hoang Nam Viet (b. 1985, Ho Chi Minh City) is a self-taught painter and multidisciplinary artist. After working in advertising, he began painting oils at 28 and committed fully to his practice at 30. He is also the founder of Hoàng Thị café, a beloved creative hub in Ho Chi Minh City.

His paintings center on the people closest to him — friends, colleagues, familiar faces — rendered with candor and warmth. Broadly expressionist in sensibility, his work resists sentimentality while affirming the depth of everyday human connection. Recent works incorporate found imagery, expanding his visual vocabulary beyond portraiture.

Hoang Nam Viet has exhibited widely across Ho Chi Minh City's contemporary art scene, including Password 0~1 at MoT+++; Lunar Breccia and Hopscotch at Galerie Quynh; The Primacy of Drawing at Dia Projects; Chung at PongDang Gallery; and Out of Nowhere at Sao La. He is represented by Galerie Quynh and lives and works in Ho Chi Minh City.
profile.welcomeBio:
Situated in the apartment building at 14 Tôn Thất Đạm — one of the city's French colonial-era buildings with classical European architecture — Hoàng Thị Studio is a uniquely personal space, shaped entirely by Hoang Nam Viet's sensibility.

The studio and accommodation are located within the Hoàng Thị café compound, on the first floor of the building. The café also houses a tattoo studio, photo booth, and a small community gathering space. The resident's area is a mezzanine directly within the café; improvements to ensure privacy and comfort will be made ahead of residencies.

Artists may choose to work in the resident space or share Hoang Nam Viet's personal studio at the rear of the café — an airy space with its own loft. Looking through the studio window, you look out onto the animated courtyard of an ancient apartment complex.
profile.collaboration: Hoang Nam Viet is genuinely open to collaborating — across mediums, across ideas, in whatever form feels right. He welcomes shared making, conversation, and critique as natural parts of living and working alongside someone.
profile.availability: Around the clock — a key will be provided.
profile.transport: 5-minute walk to bus stop, serving lines 01, 03, 04, 18, 38, 39, and 88.
profile.amenities: Located within an old apartment complex housing a cluster of cafés.
profile.rules: Bathroom is shared with Hoàng Thị café guests; in process of renovation to better accommodate resident artists.

profileVi.practiceBio:
Hoàng Nam Việt (sinh 1985, Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh) là họa sĩ tự học và nghệ sĩ đa ngành. Sau khi làm việc trong lĩnh vực quảng cáo, anh bắt đầu vẽ sơn dầu ở tuổi 28 và gắn bó toàn thời gian với hội họa ở tuổi 30. Anh cũng là người sáng lập cà phê Hoàng Thị, một không gian sáng tạo được yêu mến tại Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh.

Những bức tranh của anh tập trung vào những người thân cận nhất — bạn bè, đồng nghiệp, những khuôn mặt quen thuộc — được thể hiện với sự chân thành và ấm áp. Mang cảm thức biểu hiện rộng, tác phẩm của anh từ chối sự ủy mị trong khi khẳng định chiều sâu của những kết nối con người thường nhật. Các tác phẩm gần đây kết hợp hình ảnh tìm thấy, mở rộng ngôn ngữ thị giác của anh ra ngoài phạm vi tranh chân dung.

Hoàng Nam Việt đã triển lãm rộng rãi trong bối cảnh nghệ thuật đương đại Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, bao gồm Password 0~1 tại MoT+++; Lunar Breccia và Hopscotch tại Galerie Quynh; The Primacy of Drawing tại Dia Projects; Chung tại PongDang Gallery; và Out of Nowhere tại Sao La. Anh được đại diện bởi Galerie Quynh và sống, làm việc tại Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh.
profileVi.welcomeBio:
Tọa lạc ngay trong chung cư 14 Tôn Thất Đạm — một trong những công trình kiến trúc từ thời Pháp thuộc với lối kiến trúc Châu Âu cổ điển — Hoàng Thị Studio là một không gian vô cùng độc đáo mang đậm màu sắc cá nhân của Hoàng Nam Việt.

Studio và nơi lưu trú của nghệ sĩ nằm bên trong khuôn viên cà phê Hoàng Thị, ở tầng 1 của chung cư. Không gian cà phê còn bao gồm một studio xăm, photo booth và một không gian sinh hoạt cộng đồng nhỏ. Khu vực lưu trú là căn gác lửng ngay trong quán; các cải thiện để đảm bảo tính riêng tư và tiện nghi cho nghệ sĩ lưu trú sẽ được tiến hành trước mỗi kỳ lưu trú.

Nghệ sĩ có thể tự do lựa chọn làm việc ở không gian lưu trú hoặc chia sẻ studio cá nhân của Hoàng Nam Việt ở phía sau quán cà phê — một không gian thoáng mát với gác lửng. Từ trong studio nhìn qua khung cửa sổ, ta có thể chứng kiến sự nhộn nhịp của khu phức hợp chung cư cổ kính.
profileVi.collaboration: Hoàng Nam Việt thực sự cởi mở với sự hợp tác — dù là qua chất liệu nào, ý tưởng nào, hay hình thức nào phù hợp. Anh coi việc cùng sáng tạo, trò chuyện và trao đổi phê bình là những phần tự nhiên của việc sống và làm việc cùng nhau.
profileVi.availability: Có thể sử dụng bất cứ lúc nào — chìa khóa sẽ được cung cấp.
profileVi.neighbourhood: Quận 1, Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh
profileVi.environment: Dân sinh. Chung cư từ thời Pháp thuộc.
profileVi.transport: 5 phút đi bộ đến trạm xe buýt, phục vụ các tuyến 01, 03, 04, 18, 38, 39 và 88.
profileVi.amenities: Nằm trong khu chung cư cổ với nhiều tổ hợp quán cà phê.
profileVi.rules: Nhà vệ sinh dùng chung với khách của cà phê Hoàng Thị; đang trong quá trình cải tạo để phù hợp hơn với nghệ sĩ lưu trú.

name: P.artspace
neighborhood: Binh Thanh, Ho Chi Minh City

profile.practiceBio:
Quoc Anh Le (b. 1999, Vietnam) is a painter whose work explores the emotional terrain of love, loss, and memory. Working with a minimalist visual language, his paintings carry an intensity of feeling that belies their restraint — vulnerability rendered as form.

His series Eros, Erotoropia, Philautia, Philia, Pragma, Storge and Agápe, shown at MoT+++, maps the Greek taxonomy of love through the lens of personal experience, paying tribute to a lost relationship while connecting with a broader human condition. When in the studio, he is in the company of his pets. He has participated in group exhibitions at MoT+++, Ho Chi Minh City, where he lives and works.

Thom Nguyen (b. 1987, HCMC) is a multidisciplinary Vietnamese-American artist working across painting, sculpture, fashion, and installation. His practice investigates queerness, memory, and identity through material transformation and embodied metaphor. Drawing on diasporic histories and queer cultural forms, Nguyen's work often explores what it means to be simultaneously seen and obscured, legible and illegible.

Nguyen was a former resident at A. Farm in Ho Chi Minh City in 2025, where he developed Nước | Water Resistance, a portrait-based project examining queer identity and migration through the dual meaning of nước (water/nation). Nguyen has exhibited in Boston, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and internationally. His work is informed by speculative ritual, the politics of care, and the tension between visibility and refusal.

At the beginning of 2026, Thom and his husband Michael opened Permanently Closed — an art space and studio operating as a residency space, gallery, and private collection focused on queer artists.
profile.welcomeBio:
We welcome you to our humble art space located in the historically artsy Làng Báo Chí (press village) in Thảo Điền. Our house has a gallery and kitchen on the first floor, 2 bedrooms and an art studio with a balcony on the second, and a spacious covered rooftop lounge area on the third floor.

The first floor also features an antique Chinese noodle cart used as a bar and retail space. The exterior courtyard has lovely plants and comfortable seating for outdoor hangs, as well as a small curtained room used for performances and video installations.

Artists will be able to use the rooftop area as their studio space, as well as the second floor studio when available. The residency bedroom has large closet space, a desk, and a bed, with windows overlooking the front patio. Much of the furniture is locally sourced and handcrafted from beautiful hardwood, refurbished by local artisans. The neighbourhood is quiet and the street is lined with lush tropical plants and fruit trees.
profile.collaboration: My practice involves paintings on canvas and conceptual sculptures made by fabricators throughout the city. I would invite any resident artist to use our network of artisans to expand their practice. I am interested in portraiture and would love to work with residents to create a portrait, however that may be interpreted. Our private collection features work from mostly queer artists, and we would want to cultivate practices that explore queer identity.
profile.transport: 5-minute motorbike ride to metro station. 5-minute walk to bus station.
profile.amenities: Pharmacy at the entrance of the street. Clothing shop and cafe Objoff & Hong Cafe at the end of the street. 10-minute walk from central Thảo Điền with many shops and restaurants.
profile.residentRoom: Private bedroom with large closet, desk, and bed. Windows overlook the front patio. Not ensuite — private bathroom is separate.
profile.guestsDetail: Guests are welcome. Thom and Michael frequently host friends, artists, and collectors.
profile.rules: Quiet hours 10pm–6am. Motorbike parking available in the courtyard.
